title: Cold Email Infrastructure for Agencies — Scale Outbound for Every Client
description: Discover how agencies use ColdRelay's dedicated cold email infrastructure to run outbound campaigns across multiple clients with isolated domains, dedicated IPs, and volume pricing.

painPoints.title: Why Agencies Struggle with Cold Email Infrastructure
painPoints.content:
Agencies face a unique infrastructure challenge: every client needs their own sending infrastructure, but managing dozens of separate setups is a nightmare. The typical agency pain points include:

**Client isolation is critical.** If one client's domain gets flagged, it can't contaminate another client's reputation. Shared infrastructure creates risk that agencies can't afford.

**Scale economics don't work with retail pricing.** At $6-7/mailbox on Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 direct, managing 500-2,000 mailboxes across 10-20 clients gets prohibitively expensive. Infrastructure costs eat into margins that should be profit.

**DNS configuration is a time sink.** Setting up SPF, DKIM, and DMARC for every new client domain is manual, error-prone, and takes hours. Multiply by 20 clients and you've burned a full work week just on DNS.

**Warmup delays kill client timelines.** Clients expect results fast. Spending 2-4 weeks warming up new mailboxes before a single outreach email goes out creates frustration and delays revenue.

**Account suspensions destroy client trust.** Using Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 for cold email risks account suspension. Explaining to a client that their entire email infrastructure got shut down overnight is a relationship-ending conversation.

solution.title: How ColdRelay Solves Agency Infrastructure Challenges
solution.content:
ColdRelay is built for the agency use case. Dedicated, isolated Azure tenants mean each client's infrastructure is completely separate — no cross-contamination risk. Volume pricing from $1/mailbox (dropping to $0.55 at 5K+) makes running 500-2,000 mailboxes across your client base economically viable. Auto DNS eliminates the hours of manual SPF/DKIM/DMARC configuration. No warmup required means your clients see results from week one, not week four. And because ColdRelay is purpose-built for cold email, there's zero suspension risk — unlike Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 where a ToS violation can wipe out a client's infrastructure overnight.

For agencies, ColdRelay transforms email infrastructure from an operational headache into a competitive advantage: faster setup, lower costs, better deliverability, and zero risk of account shutdowns.

howItWorks.title: Setting Up ColdRelay for Your Agency

steps.title: Plan Client Infrastructure
steps.content: Determine the mailbox count per client based on their outreach volume. A typical client needs 50-200 mailboxes across 1-4 domains. ColdRelay's 100-150 mailboxes per domain means most clients need just 1-2 domains.

steps.title: Order and Configure
steps.content: Place your order through ColdRelay's self-service portal. Specify domains, mailbox counts, and configurations for each client. Auto DNS handles SPF, DKIM, and DMARC for every domain.

steps.title: Receive Infrastructure
steps.content: Within 2-4 hours, your client's dedicated Azure tenant is provisioned with dedicated IPs, configured mailboxes, and verified DNS. Each client's infrastructure is completely isolated.

steps.title: Connect to Sending Platform
steps.content: Import the provisioned mailboxes into your preferred sending platform — Instantly, Smartlead, or any other tool that supports SMTP/IMAP. Start campaigns immediately.

steps.title: Scale with Volume Pricing
steps.content: As you onboard more clients, your per-mailbox cost decreases. At 200+ total mailboxes you're at $0.85 each. At 1,000+ you're at $0.70. At 5,000+ you hit $0.55 — your margins improve as you grow.

items.title: Client Isolation Guaranteed
items.content: Each client runs on their own isolated Azure tenant with dedicated IPs. A deliverability issue with one client never affects another. This is the #1 infrastructure requirement for agencies managing multiple brands.

items.title: Volume Economics
items.content: At $0.55/mailbox (5K+ tier), running 100 mailboxes per client across 50 clients costs $2,750/month. The same setup on Google Workspace would cost $35,000+/month. That margin difference is pure profit.

items.title: Same-Day Client Onboarding
items.content: New client signs on Monday? Their infrastructure is live by Monday afternoon. No 2-4 week warmup delays. Campaigns start immediately with ColdRelay's pre-optimized infrastructure.

items.title: Zero Suspension Risk
items.content: Purpose-built cold email infrastructure means no ToS violations, no surprise suspensions, no awkward calls to clients explaining why their email is down.

items.title: Simplified Operations
items.content: Auto DNS eliminates hours of manual configuration per client. Consistent infrastructure across all clients reduces troubleshooting complexity and training requirements.

faqs.question: Can each client have their own isolated infrastructure?
faqs.answer: Yes. ColdRelay provisions each order on its own isolated Azure tenant with dedicated IPs. Client A's infrastructure is completely separate from Client B's — no shared resources, no reputation cross-contamination.

faqs.question: How does agency pricing work with ColdRelay?
faqs.answer: ColdRelay's volume pricing applies to your total mailbox count across all clients. As your agency grows, per-mailbox costs decrease: $1 (1-199), $0.85 (200-999), $0.70 (1K-4,999), $0.55 (5K+).

faqs.question: What happens if a client churns?
faqs.answer: Shut down their mailboxes and stop paying for them. ColdRelay bills monthly with no long-term commitments — you're not locked into infrastructure for clients who leave.

faqs.question: Can I white-label ColdRelay for my clients?
faqs.answer: ColdRelay provides the infrastructure behind the scenes. Your clients see their own domains and mailboxes — ColdRelay isn't visible to them. You can position the infrastructure as part of your agency's service.

faqs.question: How many mailboxes should I set up per client?
faqs.answer: It depends on outreach volume. Most agencies start with 50-100 mailboxes per client (sending 2-5 emails/day per mailbox). High-volume clients may need 200-500 mailboxes. ColdRelay supports 100-150 mailboxes per domain.
